svg.text: Cyclic Sort — Place Each Value at Its Index
Input: array containing integers in 1..n (or 0..n-1) with at most one
Idea: each value v belongs at index v - 1 (or v).
Walk the array; while nums[i] is not at its correct index, swap it home.
Each swap places one value forever. Total swaps ≤ n. O(n) time, O(1) space.
After the pass, missing / duplicated values reveal themselves by mismatch.
• Input is a permutation or near-permutation of 1..n / 0..n-1
• Question asks for missing, duplicate, or first absent positive
• Constraint: O(1) extra space requested explicitly
• Hashset or counting array would solve it in O(n) — but uses O(n) memory
Cyclic sort is the in-place trick that beats hashing on memory while
If values are bounded by index range, cyclic sort.
